mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS level relative to its 6-month moving average signals reduced discount-rate pressure on equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This is an always-on trend effect independent of any threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive-return hit rate in declining-rate months is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window (a Sahm-rule-style trigger) signals an emerging labor-market downturn that is not yet priced into equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-10 vintage, near multi-year lows but trending up from 2023 trough
failure_condition: Among triggered instances, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth combined with a still-restrictive FEDFUNDS level indicates the Fed is holding rates high into a weakening labor market, amplifying downside risk to equities beyond what either series predicts alone, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.83% as of 2024-10 vintage while payroll growth has been moderating
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor slowdown
failure_condition: The joint-condition subsample shows no significant difference in mean forward returns versus either single-factor subsample alone (interaction term insignificant at p > 0.10 in regression), falsifying the claimed interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation combined with a high FEDFUNDS rate, equity markets price in sustained restrictive policy, producing negative forward returns; but once inflation decelerates toward target while rates stay elevated, this negative relationship reverses because markets anticipate imminent easing.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 315.631 as of 2024-10 vintage, FEDFUNDS at 4.83%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in the high-inflation/high-rate regime are not significantly more negative than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05), or the sign flips inconsistently across at least two distinct historical tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week. This is a continuous always-on trend signal with no activation threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 708290 million as of 2024-10 vintage
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month momentum and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO of more than 1% over a trailing 3-month period signals manufacturing sector contraction that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 99.4695 vs vintage 102.2805 as of 2024-10, showing a large downward data revision
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) weakness combined with elevated FEDFUNDS jointly signal that high borrowing costs are suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand, compounding into broader equity market weakness beyond what either series indicates alone, over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1357k units as of 2024-09 vintage, FEDFUNDS at 4.83%
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-low/FEDFUNDS-high subsample does not produce forward returns significantly more negative than either factor's single-condition subsample (interaction coefficient p > 0.10), rejecting the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where GDPC1 growth is decelerating toward recessionary territory, a rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth amplifies negative equity forward returns because labor deterioration confirms rather than merely signals demand contraction; outside of such a growth-slowdown regime, the same labor signals have muted predictive power.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising and PAYEMS growth decelerating while trailing GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised to 23478.57 billion as of 2024-07 vintage
failure_condition: During periods classified as growth-slowdown regime, the labor-signal-triggered 60-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than labor-signal-triggered returns in expansionary regimes (regime interaction term p > 0.10), invalidating the regime-conditional claim.
